title: Whitelist Certain Devices/Device Types
author: marius137
body:
I own a Quest 2 so I constantly get alerts from it.
Being able to whitelist certain MAC-Addresses so I don't get alerts from my own devices would be nice.
If it is not possible to whitelist single Devices filtering by category would work. I am much more paranoid about smart glasses than VR headsets

Edit: I noticed that "Add an option to set false positives to an ignore list. Maybe in the notification?" is already in the todo, but the point about filtering by device type should still be valid

title: Community Effort: Scan for more manufacturers!
author: yjeanrenaud
body:
I am aware that there are more Smart Glasses out there than those we already try to spot with these apps. Currently, I am aware of XGIMI and Rollme as well as Samsung that have announced or already launched their own smart glasses with recording functionalities.
It would be great to include them in the app, too.
Hence, I call the public to use the app in debug mode (instead of canary mode) when near such a device, e.g. at a sales point, and share the logs with the community.  You might just copypaste these logs here. They do not contain very sensitive information and are not linked to you or your phone. They only list the manufacturer's numbers, time and date, as well as the signal strength. 

Hopefully, we will be able to identify them as well. Thanks in advance

title: Stops scanning after screen rotation
author: xandro0777
body: .. or at least it appears so.

title: Wrong signing key?
author: IzzySoft
body:
Looks like with v1.0.6, the signing key has changed:

```
2026-03-26 20:04:43,958 WARNING: "ch.pocketpc.nearbyglasses_7.apk" is signed by a key that is not allowed:
375b9b299c74c17b2c9d6a17236055a64b69bb6de11e181cf1081071862506a6
```

so this update was rejected. What happened? Changing the signing key makes updates impossible – as the new APK would be rejected, for security reasons.

Oh! That happened:

    Signer #1 certificate DN: C=US, O=Android, CN=Android Debug

you've signed the APK using a debug key. Probably by accident, as those are not intended for release builds. Can you please fix that?

title: Build of v.1.0.5 fails
author: IzzySoft
body:
Doesn't even start to build, that is:

```
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* Where:
Build file '/build/repo/build.gradle' line: 2

* What went wrong:
Plugin [id: 'com.android.application'] was not found in any of the following sources:
  
- Gradle Core Plugins (plugin is not in 'org.gradle' namespace)
- Included Builds (No included builds contain this plugin)
- Plugin Repositories (plugin dependency must include a version number for this source)
```

Any clue what could be amiss there? Must have been available with your build, as otherwise you'd have no APK for it… Could it be that the `build.gradle` in your project root lacks specifying the version, like

```gradle
plugins {
    id 'com.android.application' version '8.13.2'
}
```

while you locally have it? At v.1.0.4, you had that as well:

```gradle
plugins {
    id 'com.android.application' version '8.13.2' apply false
    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' version '1.9.22' apply false
}
```

PS: looks like you accidentally copied `app/build.gradle` to the project root, could that be?

PPS: what is the difference between the 2 APKs at v.1.0.5? Should we pick the other one now? It would also help a lot if you'd build within the git repo, and not from a copy not having the `.git/` directory. That missing, makes debugging failed builds harder…

title: Add a link for obtainium based app installs.
author: monksy
body:
Add a link to allow the user to install this via obtainum. 

I would create a PR for this, but the CLA is a no go for me.

title: Option to start on Boot
author: xandro0777
body: Probably should have an option to start on boot?

title: Scanning disabled after a while despite notification saying it is still active
author: xandro0777
body:
I have started scanning and the notification saying scanning for smart glasses is active but when I open the app from launcher after some longer time the red button says "start scanning" thus indicating otherwise.

Foreground service is enabled.

The weird thing, the system even displays the app as running

title: UI Issue: Content cut off on Samsung S25 (Base model) - No scrolling available
author: t3nt4k
body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1667b642-6b34-45e3-a123-38de5ebfb5b8)

Hi,

I'm using the app on a Samsung Galaxy S25 (base model) and I've encountered a display issue. The bottom of the main page is cut off, and I cannot scroll down to see the rest of the information or access the debug log area.

Device: Samsung Galaxy S25 / Android 16
App version: 1.0.4

Issue: The UI doesn't fit the screen height and there is no ScrollView implemented to reach the bottom content.

Screenshot: (I recommend attaching the image you shared here to your GitHub issue).

Could you please look into making the main view scrollable or adjusting the layout for smaller screen factors?

Best regards,

title: CHECK_LICENSE Permission
author: Quantum-Future
body:
I recently found this interesting app !
It's a free app, right?  So, why does it need the CHECK_LICENSE permission?
https://reports.exodus-privacy.eu.org/es/reports/ch.pocketpc.nearbyglasses/latest/

title: Stops detecting when glasses connected to phone
author: candiesdoodle2
body:
Trying to test this app for detection in a secure work enviornment.
During my testing i noticed that the app stops detecting the glasses, when the glasses are connected to a seperate phone via bluetooth. Possibly because the BLE adv beacons are not sent anymore?
I have tested with one pair, will test with another one and share feedback.
But if anyone has any feedback or experience to share, that will be helpful!

title: feature request: custom app notification sound and vibrations
author: ThisisShashwat
body: doing this will be fairly easy and really helpful in recognising this apps notification sound over the other apps as I usually dont check my notifications the moment I see it

title: Add themed icon.
author: dav23r
body:
Suggesting to add monochrome layer to the app icon in order for it to be themable on modern Android versions.

For reference: https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/launch/icon_design_adaptive

title: No scrolling on main screen possible in landscape
author: der-webtracker
body: When in landscape view I can't scroll down and see the debug log. I can see everything in portrait so I can't tell if that would make scrolling possible. At least on my phone I would not be able to tell if the scan is really working when being in landscape. Scrolling in the settings list is fine, though. Phone: Pixel 7 Pro (Android 16) App version: 1.0.4

title: Top bar stuck under android UI
author: rmsa5
body: On Android 16, the app's top bar (that includes icons such as settings) are stuck under the Android UI (time, various notification icons, wifi, mobile connectivity...) and is irresponsive to touch inputs by user. See screenshot

title: App icon not displaying in Settings menu
author: TheJessieKirk
body:
The icon displays correctly on my launcher but does not display at all in any of the Settings menu screens.

I'm using LineageOS 23.2 (Android 16) on a Sony Xperia XA2 Ultra.

title: Feature Request: iOS Support
author: valentinlamine
body:
I saw iOS support mentioned in the README and I'm very interested in this feature.

I’m a developer (new to Swift, but experienced in other fields) and I have a Mac available for builds and testing. I’d be happy to help you prototype or test an iOS version if you're looking for contributors.
